title: Unit 0: Java Basics
description: Get started with the fundamentals of Java syntax, including variables, control structures, standard input/output, and basic structures.

topics.title: Structure & Standard I/O
topics.description: Every Java program requires a class and a main method. Standard Input is handled using the Scanner class, and output is sent to System.out.

ispo.desc: Reads an integer from standard input using the scanner and stores it in number1.
Reads another integer from standard input using the scanner and stores it in number2.
Creates the inputReader object of type Scanner to store system input streams in memory.
Declares and allocates memory for number1 (int).
Declares and allocates memory for number2 (int).
Declares sumResult variable to store the result of the addition.
Performs the arithmetic addition of number1 and number2.
Prints the prompt message to screen requesting first number.
Prints the prompt message to screen requesting second number.
Outputs the string concatenation and the calculated sumResult to standard output.

quiz.q: Which class in Java is commonly used to read input from standard input (keyboard)?

topics.title: Control Flow & Arrays
topics.description: Control flow defines the execution path (if-else, loops). Arrays are fixed-size sequential memory blocks that store elements of the same type.

ispo.desc: Receives the reference to the array 'numbers' as an input parameter.
Declares maxVal and allocates memory to store the candidate maximum value.
Iterates through array indices 1 to length-1, comparing elements and conditionally updating maxVal.
Returns maxVal, passing the output result back to the calling function.

quiz.q: What is the index of the last element in an array named 'values' of size 10?
